_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Ureteroscopy, Stone removal with lithotripsy - Upper Ureter
clinical_indications: Colicky pain starting from back and radiating to front (suggestive of upper ureteric stone)
Haematuria may be present with stone at any location
Colics may be associated with nausea and vomiting
Can present as anuria in bilateral ureteric stones or ureteric stone in a solitary kidney

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment
IVP / NCCT+ CT-IVP confirming the indication for the procedure

claim.label: Post procedure Imaging X Ray/USG showing stone is removed.

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the IVP / NCCT/ CT-IVP confirming the indication for the procedure submitted?
Was the Clinical notes and IVP / NCCT/ CT-IVP indicative of procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Was the Post procedure Imaging X Ray/USG showing the stone removed?
Was the Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes submitted?
Was the Detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes and IVP / NCCT/ CT-IVP not indicative of the procedure
Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment missing or incomplete at pre-authorization
IVP / NCCT+ CT-IVP confirming the indication for the procedure missing or inadequate at pre-authorization
Detailed indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details missing or incomplete at claim submission
Post-procedure imaging (X-Ray/USG) not showing stone removal at claim submission
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes missing or incomplete at claim submission
Detailed discharge summary missing or incomplete at claim submission

clinical_key_pointers: Colic pain radiating from back to front
Haematuria may be present or absent
May be associated with fever, nausea and vomiting
History of site & type of pain, associated symptoms hint towards possible site of stones. Remember to take past history of stone diseases, medications, family history etc.
